# Self-Review Checklist: Orchestrated Spec-Kit Command Workflow Platform

**Purpose**: Provide a strict, author-facing checklist that reviews whether the feature requirements are complete, precise, internally consistent, and ready for implementation across workflow control, review gates, artifact persistence, recovery, and operator transparency.
**Created**: 2026-03-25
**Feature**: [spec.md](D:/资料/资料/公司/个人/test/ai-agent-playground/spec-kit_project/spec-kit-project/specs/001-multi-agent-specflow/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are requirements explicitly defined for the full operator flow from run creation to command selection, agent dispatch, review, local artifact finalization, and recovery without relying on implied behavior? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK002 - Are prerequisite-validation requirements documented for both allowed and blocked command requests, including what evidence must be retained in each case? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-025, Data Model §CommandExecution]
- [x] CHK003 - Are review requirements complete for every artifact class that can influence later workflow stages, including generated docs, prerequisite artifacts, and implementation-heavy outputs? [Completeness, Spec §Agent Workflow & Review, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §ReviewDecision]
- [x] CHK004 - Are post-review write-finalization requirements defined for every outcome path, including full success, partial success, retryable failure, and operator-visible incomplete recovery? [Completeness, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Spec §FR-026, Spec §FR-032]
- [x] CHK005 - Are requirements complete for what operators must be able to see in timeline/history views about command state, review state, write state, recovery action, and dependency failure cause? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-011, Contract §CommandExecutionSummary]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 - Is the uniqueness scope of `feature_key` precise enough to remove ambiguity across reruns, repeated feature names, and multiple workflows in the same workspace? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Data Model §WorkflowRun]
- [x] CHK007 - Is the distinction between `blocked`, `failed`, `review_required`, `incomplete`, and `completed` stated with enough precision that implementations would not invent different interpretations? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-016, Data Model §State Transitions]
- [x] CHK008 - Is the source-of-truth rule for local artifacts specific enough to resolve conflicts between stored metadata, content hash, and filesystem timestamps without extra assumptions? [Clarity, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Spec §FR-023]
- [x] CHK009 - Is “important artifacts” defined with enough specificity that two reviewers would agree which artifacts must create `ReviewDecision` records and which may remain transient? [Clarity, Spec §Agent Workflow & Review, Data Model §ReviewDecision]
- [x] CHK010 - Are the terms “communication records,” “handoff payloads,” “execution summaries,” and “derived history views” clear enough to avoid overlapping storage responsibilities? [Clarity, Spec §Agent Workflow & Review, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §CommunicationEnvelope]
- [x] CHK011 - Is the meaning of “stable output path” clear enough to distinguish path stability from version stability, backup retention, and current-version pointers? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-015a, Data Model §ArtifactDocument]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 - Do the user stories, functional requirements, data model, and API contract remain consistent about reruns creating new `CommandExecution` records rather than mutating previous attempts? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-024, Data Model §CommandExecution, Contract §CommandExecutionSummary]
- [x] CHK013 - Do the requirements stay consistent about MySQL being the system of record while local files remain the operator-facing source package between commands? [Consistency, Spec §Technical Constraints, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Data Model §Durability, Privacy, And Recovery Rules]
- [x] CHK014 - Are review-gate requirements consistent about when a command may be shown as complete versus incomplete after approvals, revisions, blocked decisions, or write-finalization failures? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-026, Data Model §CommandExecution]
- [x] CHK015 - Do timeline/history requirements align across spec, plan, and contract so there is one unambiguous set of fields operators must be able to inspect? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §Summary, Contract §WorkflowRunDetail]
- [x] CHK016 - Are retention, privacy, and auditability rules consistent with the prohibition on storing secrets in communication history or other persisted workflow records? [Consistency, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Spec §FR-029, Data Model §CommunicationEnvelope]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 - Can the command-availability, prerequisite-validation, and audit lookup performance requirements be objectively measured from the written criteria without inventing extra thresholds? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-004, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK018 - Is success for rerun traceability measurable enough to determine whether overwrite behavior, version lineage, and backup handling are sufficient? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007, Spec §FR-015a]
- [x] CHK019 - Are incomplete recovery outcomes measurable enough that an operator can objectively determine when a write retry is required instead of a rerun? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008, Spec §FR-032, Contract §CommandExecutionSummary]
- [x] CHK020 - Can the auditability requirement be verified from the documented history fields alone, including command, agent, model, review, source-of-truth, and output path lineage? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004, Spec §FR-010, Data Model §Relationship Summary]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK021 - Are requirements defined for the zero-history start state where a workflow exists but no commands, artifacts, or review decisions have been created yet? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Data Model §WorkflowRun]
- [x] CHK022 - Are requirements specified for blocked-prerequisite scenarios in enough detail to define operator messaging, persisted evidence, and command state? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-025]
- [x] CHK023 - Are manual-edit scenarios fully covered, including the case where a user changes a local artifact after approval but before the next command snapshots input state? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK024 - Are mixed-history scenarios covered where a workflow contains completed commands, blocked commands, failed commands, and incomplete write-recovery commands simultaneously? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §WorkflowRunDetail]
- [x] CHK025 - Are requirements defined for starting another workflow with the same human-readable feature name in the same workspace after a prior run completes or is abandoned? [Coverage, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Data Model §WorkflowRun]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK026 - Are overwrite failure requirements complete for the case where backup creation fails before the target file is replaced? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §ArtifactVersion]
- [x] CHK027 - Are recovery requirements complete for the case where overwrite succeeds but version metadata or backup registration fails afterward? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-026, Data Model §ArtifactVersion]
- [x] CHK028 - Are fallback-model requirements specified for the case where the preferred model is unavailable and no valid fallback remains? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-031, Data Model §StageExecution]
- [x] CHK029 - Are database outage requirements defined for crashes or outages that happen after review approval but before local file finalization completes? [Edge Case, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Decision 10]
- [x] CHK030 - Are requirements explicit for hash-match but metadata-stale reconciliation cases, where timestamps alone might otherwise cause conflicting source-of-truth decisions? [Edge Case, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Data Model §ArtifactDocument]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK031 - Are retention requirements documented with enough precision to determine how long workflow history and superseded backups must remain available? [Non-Functional, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Spec §FR-028]
- [x] CHK032 - Are privacy constraints specific enough to define which workflow records may store summaries and refs but must exclude credentials, tokens, and other secret material? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-029, Data Model §CommunicationEnvelope]
- [x] CHK033 - Are indexing and query obligations defined clearly enough to support availability checks, prerequisite validation, and artifact audit lookups at the expected scale? [Non-Functional, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §Indexing And Query Obligations]
- [x] CHK034 - Are durability requirements precise enough to determine the mandatory ordering between persisted state transitions and filesystem writes? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-030, Data Model §Durability, Privacy, And Recovery Rules, Research §Decision 3]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK035 - Are assumptions about Hugging Face availability, fallback exhaustion, and MySQL availability documented in a way that makes dependency-caused state transitions explicit? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-031, Research §Decision 10]
- [x] CHK036 - Are assumptions about one workspace at a time, dozens of runs per day, and hundreds of revisions translated into concrete design expectations rather than left as narrative context only? [Assumption, Plan §Technical Context, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules]
- [x] CHK037 - Are workspace-boundary requirements explicit enough to define what counts as an allowed local workspace and which artifact paths must be rejected? [Dependency, Spec §Technical Constraints, Data Model §WorkflowRun, Data Model §ArtifactDocument]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK038 - Do the requirements cleanly separate “retry write finalization” from “rerun command,” or could a reader still confuse these as the same recovery action? [Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-026, Contract §CommandExecutionSummary]
- [x] CHK039 - Is there any remaining conflict between “one command at a time” workflow control and the need to keep blocked or incomplete attempts visible while a newer rerun becomes the active command? [Conflict, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-024, Data Model §WorkflowRun]
- [x] CHK040 - Are there any unresolved conflicts between canonical persisted history, rollback history, and operator timeline views that could lead to divergent interpretations of the same command attempt? [Conflict, Spec §FR-027, Spec §Persistence, History, And Recovery Rules, Data Model §Persistence Classification]

## Notes

- Focus areas: end-to-end workflow requirements, review gating, artifact lineage, recovery semantics, auditability, and operator visibility
- Depth: strict
- Audience: author self-check
- Timing: before implementation expands beyond the current design baseline, and again before closing the design phase
- This checklist is intentionally broader than a single-domain checklist and is meant to act as a high-rigor self-review gate for requirements quality
- Reviewed on 2026-03-25 against spec.md, plan.md, data-model.md, esearch.md, and contracts/workflow-api.yaml; all items were confirmed as passed under a strict author self-review after aligning contract timeline fields and workspace-boundary rules.
